question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified() in CutoverServiceImpl] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes verified() to be called with a RecordFile that has a null or zero consensusStart, causing lastSwitchedOrVerified.set(System.currentTimeMillis()) to be updated even though no real progress was made, resetting the threshold timer and preventing the system from switching stream types when it should?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onExit() in ShutdownHelper] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the JVM to receive a shutdown signal (e.g., OOM killer during partition-induced resource exhaustion), causing ShutdownHelper.onExit() to set stopping=true and sleep 10 seconds, but if the download thread is blocked on a network call, it won't check isStopping() and the process terminates with in-flight data partially written?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isStopping() in ShutdownHelper] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles() loop to check ShutdownHelper.isStopping() only at the top of each iteration, and if a network partition causes a single iteration to block for an extended period (e.g., waiting for S3 timeout), the shutdown check is never reached, preventing graceful shutdown?
